name: Reka Core
archDisplay: Dense transformer, PaLM-like (no parallel layers); full architecture undisclosed
note: Reka's frontier multimodal model (text/image/video/audio); approached GPT-4V/Claude-3-Opus class on multimodal + language benchmarks at launch.

results.notes: Table 5 of the Reka Core/Flash/Edge technical report.
Table 13 (medical reasoning benchmarks); vs Meditron-70B 79.8, Med-PaLM-2 79.2, GPT-4 80.4 in the same table.

Reported as "GPQA-main (Hard QA)" in Table 5 of the Reka Core/Flash/Edge technical report; also appears in the Apr 15, 2024 press release table. vs GPT-4 38.1, Claude 3 Opus 50.2, Claude 3 Sonnet 39.1, Gemini Ultra 35.7, Gemini Pro 1.5 41.5. Not previously in the corpus despite being in the same source table as the model's existing MMLU/GSM8K/HumanEval results.

Table 11 (cross-lingual evaluations), 0-shot accuracy, mean across 6 languages (fr, en, jp, pt, zh, ru). vs GPT-4 91.5, Reka Flash 84.0, Llama-2 70B 65.3, GPT-3.5 72.2. Reka Core underperforms GPT-4 here per the paper's own discussion (one of only two multilingual tasks where Core trails GPT-4).

verificationNotes: CONFIRMED. reka.ai blog post body dated Apr 15, 2024 confirms Core's launch, 128K context, 32 languages, and MMMU 56.3 near GPT-4V 56.8. arxiv 2404.12387v1 (Reka Core/Flash/Edge tech report, submitted Apr 18 2024) confirms all three benchmark rows exactly: MMLU 83.2, GSM8K 92.2, HumanEval 76.8. Current docs.reka.ai/pricing confirms $2/$6 per-million-token pricing exactly matches the record (launch-day press-release pricing of $10/$25 was superseded and is no longer current, but current live pricing matches record so no change needed). No corrections required.
